target.name: Bedrock Energy
target.founder: Joselyn Lai, Silviu Livescu, Joel Wish

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Bedrock Energy is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Joselyn Lai, Silviu Livescu, and Joel Wish, having raised $15M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers (including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base ($15M), applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to later-stage advancement, implying a $30M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $150M (range: $110M–$200M). These peers—Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M—reflect recent market pricing in the climate and energy space at equivalent development stages.

The dual-method triangulation produces a range of $26M to $172M, with a median cross-section of $90M. The wide spread reflects material tension between the stage-progression method (asset-light, capital-efficient view) and peer multiples (market-based, sector momentum). Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three peer comps, which may embed sector-specific valuation premiums, and whether Bedrock's technology profile and capital efficiency track closer to early-stage stage baselines or to later-stage funded peer valuations. Additional clarity on revenue traction, IP position, and pathway to Series B capital efficiency would narrow this range.
